sourceMovie.title: Last Night in Soho
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Last Night in Soho works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: It Follows
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: It Follows (2015) is the kind of film Last Night in Soho fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The horror overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Suspiria
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Suspiria shares Last Night in Soho's gift for horror — the 2018 film delivers mystery without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Midnight Meat Train
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Last Night in Soho compelling — the horror, the mystery — shows up in The Midnight Meat Train (2008) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Dressed to Kill
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror energy of Last Night in Soho is alive in Dressed to Kill (1980), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Oddity
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Oddity (2024) operates in the same horror as Last Night in Soho, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Psycho II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Psycho II (1983) belongs to the same conversation as Last Night in Soho — both anchor their horror in mystery emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: House on Haunted Hill
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Last Night in Soho's horror stayed with you, House on Haunted Hill (1959) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The mystery through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Sherlock Holmes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Last Night in Soho excels in mystery, Sherlock Holmes (2009) doubles down on adventure to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Conjuring 2 (2016) is the natural next step after Last Night in Soho: same horror DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and craft elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Child's Play
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Last Night in Soho who respond to its horror will find Child's Play (1988) immediately familiar. Both films share a dark sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

faq.question: What should I watch after Last Night in Soho?
faq.answer: Try It Follows, Suspiria, The Midnight Meat Train—each shares DNA with Last Night in Soho while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Last Night in Soho?
faq.answer: Last Night in Soho (2021) is best described as Horror, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Last Night in Soho worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
